evals.prompt: The src/inference/ directory is getting really messy. We have vdb_sync, orphan_resolver, lm_link_inferencer, and a bunch of relationship normalization logic all in separate files. Where should I start cleaning this up?
evals.expected_output: Reads graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md and relevant src/inference/ files. Identifies shallow pass-through modules vs. ones earning their keep. Presents a numbered list of deepening candidates with Files/Problem/Solution/Benefits using the LANGUAGE.md vocabulary (depth, seam, locality, leverage). Does NOT propose interfaces — asks which candidate to explore.

evals.prompt: What architectural improvements would make the Theseus codebase more testable? I feel like a lot of the tests are fragile and tied to implementation details.
evals.expected_output: Reads graphify dependency map first, then explores src/. Identifies modules where tests cross the wrong seam (testing past the interface). Applies the deletion test to suspect shallow modules. Presents candidates with benefits framed in terms of testability improvement (fewer brittle mocks, tests that survive refactors). Uses LANGUAGE.md vocabulary throughout.

evals.prompt: We keep adding inference algorithms to the post-processing pipeline. The six-phase structure in src/inference/ is getting hard to navigate. Should we consolidate anything?
evals.expected_output: Explores the six-phase pipeline structure. Identifies coupling between phases and where phase boundaries might be the wrong seam. Classifies dependencies (in-process vs. external like Neo4j). Proposes deepening candidates (e.g. merging phases with high coupling into a single deep module with a cleaner interface). Records any rejected candidates as ADR candidates if warranted.

evals.prompt: The prompt registration system feels scattered — prompts/govcon_prompt.py, prompts/extraction/, prompts/multimodal/, and src/server/initialization.py all have to be kept in sync. Is there a better structure?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the multi-file prompt registration as a shallow cluster where the seam is in the wrong place. Applies deletion test: would deleting the current registration indirection concentrate complexity? Presents a deepening candidate that unifies the prompt lifecycle behind a single interface. Surfaces the existing 3-prompt-system constraint from ARCHITECTURE.md as context.
